title: ESP32-CAM OV2640 WiFi Camera Module
design_question: How to capture and stream live video from an embedded device over WiFi with optional face-detection AI?
selected_approach: ESP32-CAM module (AI-Thinker variant: ESP32-S, OV2640, 4 MB PSRAM, microSD slot). CameraWebServer example (Espressif ESP32 Arduino). Resolution: SVGA 800×600 for streaming, UXGA 1600×1200 for still capture. Flash LED GPIO 4 (HIGH = on). USB-UART for programming: IO0 → GND for flash mode. 5 V / 500 mA PSU minimum. JPEG quality 10 for MJPEG stream. PIR on GPIO 13 for motion-triggered capture in deep-sleep mode.
rationale: ESP32-CAM provides WiFi + camera + AI inference in a < £5 module. PSRAM is built-in enabling SVGA+ resolution. The official CameraWebServer example provides a fully functional MJPEG stream and face-detection UI with no custom driver work. Deep-sleep + PIR wake gives < 0.5 mA standby for battery-powered cameras.

alternatives.approach: Raspberry Pi Zero W + camera module
alternatives.reason_rejected: RPi Zero gives higher resolution and easier Python OpenCV, but runs Linux (30 s boot), costs 5×, and uses 200+ mA idle — ESP32-CAM better for always-on embedded use.

alternatives.approach: OV7670 + Arduino Mega (no PSRAM, no WiFi)
alternatives.reason_rejected: Requires frame buffer in Arduino SRAM (too small for QVGA+); no WiFi; poor frame rate. ESP32-CAM far superior.

verification_notes: FACT: 'ESP32-CAM — OV2640 Camera Module with WiFi Streaming' confirms SVGA 12 fps, PSRAM requirement, GPIO 4 flash, and PIR deep-sleep strategy.
supporting_fact_titles: ESP32-CAM — OV2640 Camera Module with WiFi Streaming
